title: Integrity Code Series - Week 8 - High-Temperature Creep-Fatigue Interaction in Fired Heater Tubes
description:
Coupled creep-fatigue degradation simulator for fired-heater tubes in 9Cr-1Mo-V-Nb steel (ASME Grade T91 / P91). Implements Norton power-law secondary creep, MPC Omega tertiary creep (API 579-1 Part 10), Larson-Miller minimum stress (API 530), parabolic Wagner oxide growth, Coffin-Manson low-cycle fatigue, and the ASME Section III Division 5 bilinear creep-fatigue interaction envelope for Gr91. The coupling is physical: oxide growth thins the wall, thinning increases hoop stress, higher stress accelerates creep, and combined damage is checked against the envelope.

Anchored to Marathon Martinez CSB Report (2025-03-13) and API STD 560:2026. 324 tests. Result: a 40C hot spot increases creep damage by 15x.
references: API STD 530 (2023). Calculation of Heater-Tube Thickness in Petroleum Refineries.
API 579-1 / ASME FFS-1 (2016). Fitness-for-Service, Part 10 Creep.
ASME (2019). ASME Section III Division 5: High Temperature Reactors.
API 571 (2020). Damage Mechanisms Affecting Fixed Equipment in the Refining Industry.
API STD 560 (2026). Fired Heaters for General Refinery Service.
U.S. Chemical Safety Board (2025). Marathon Martinez Refinery Heater Tube Rupture, Report 2025-03-13.
NIMS Data Sheet 43A. Creep rupture of 9Cr-1Mo-V-Nb steel.
Wagner C. (1933). Beitrag zur Theorie des Anlaufvorgangs. Zeitschrift fur Physikalische Chemie 21B: 25-41.
